sections.paragraph:
m. (once f. Mic 6:9), pl. מַטֹּות Num 1:16 Jos 14:1, Jos 14:2once with suff. מַטָּיו Hab 3:14.

(1) a branch, a twig, so called from the idea of stretching out (from the root נָטָה, compare נְטִישָׁה, from נָטַשׁ), Eze 19:11, seqq.

(2) a rod, a staff, Exo 4:2, , 17 Num 20:9. The phrase שָׁבַר מַטֵּה־לֶחֶם to break the staff of bread, is i.q. to cause a want of bread, i.e. famine, as bread is in the Hebrew called the stay of the heart (compare סָעַד), Lev 26:26 Psa 105:16 Eze 4:16 5:16 14:13 . Specially used of the rod of a king, a sceptre, Psa 110:2 of a soldier’s spear, Hab 3:9, 14 Hab 3:14 1Sa 14:27 of a rod used in chastising, Isa 9:3, מַטֵּה שִׁכְמֹו “the rod which smote his back;” Isa 10:5, 24 Isa 10:24 Eze 7:11, “violence has risen up לְמַטֵּה־רֶשַׁע to chasten wickedness;” whence used of the punishment itself, Mic 6:9.

(3) i.q. שֵׁבֶט a tribe (prop. branch) of the people, only used of the tribes of Israel, as מַטֵּה לֵוִי Num 1:49 מַטֵּה בְנֵי מְנַשֶּׁה the tribe of the children of Manasseh, Jos 13:29 רָאשֵׁי הַמַּטֹּות 1Ki 8:1, the leaders of the tribes.
